target.title: fix(imessage): detect and recover anchorless watch payloads before routing
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix iMessage group link-preview watch payloads that lose their conversation anchor (`chat_id=0`, empty `chat_guid` / `chat_identifier`, `is_group=false`) before they can be classified as sender DMs.
- Move anchorless recovery/fail-closed drop ahead of live inbound debounce classification so `coalesceSameSenderDms` cannot merge malformed group payloads under the raw `chat:0` DM bucket.
- Keep the existing recovery path for catchup/direct handling and add monitor-level regression coverage for the live coalescing path.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: iMessage group link-preview watch payloads can arrive without usable conversation anchor fields. Before this fix, live notifications entered the inbound debouncer first, so with `coalesceSameSenderDms=true` multiple anchorless group payloads from the same sender could be merged under `imessage:<account>:dm:chat:0:<sender>` before recovery. The merged turn was then repaired using only the first GUID and could route content from another group under the first recovered group.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.4.1, Node.js v24.15.0, OpenClaw v2026.5.19, worktree SHA `e22876fd1bef`, `imsg` 0.9.0 installed at `/opt/homebrew/bin/imsg`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
imsg status --json
printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"chats.list","params":{"limit":3}}\n' | imsg rpc --json

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19046 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=pr-84503-local-token \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-pr-84503-local-proof/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-pr-84503-local-proof/state/openclaw.json \
node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway --bind custom --port 19046

curl --noproxy '*' -sS -I http://127.0.0.1:19046/
curl --noproxy '*' -sS http://127.0.0.1:19046/health

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19046 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=pr-84503-local-token \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-pr-84503-local-proof/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-pr-84503-local-proof/state/openclaw.json \
node scripts/run-node.mjs channels status --probe --channel imessage --json

pnpm exec vitest run \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor.watch-subscribe-retry.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
$ imsg status --json
{
  "version": "0.9.0",
  "basic_features": true,
  "sip": "enabled",
  "advanced_features": false,
  "rpc_methods": [
    "chats.list",
    "messages.history",
    "watch.subscribe",
    "watch.unsubscribe",
    "send",
    "send.rich",
    "send.attachment"
  ]
}

$ printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"chats.list","params":{"limit":3}}\n' | imsg rpc --json
{"result":{"chats":[]},"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}
```

```text
2026-05-24T18:23:21.205+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, imessage, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 1.7s)
2026-05-24T18:23:21.211+08:00 [gateway] starting channels and sidecars...
2026-05-24T18:23:21.264+08:00 [imessage] [default] starting provider (/opt/homebrew/bin/imsg db=/Users/zhangguiping/Library/Messages/chat.db)
2026-05-24T18:23:21.646+08:00 [gateway] ready
```

```text
$ curl --noproxy '*' -sS -I http://127.0.0.1:19046/
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8

$ curl --noproxy '*' -sS http://127.0.0.1:19046/health
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs channels status --probe --channel imessage --json
{
  "channels": {
    "imessage": {
      "configured": true,
      "cliPath": "/opt/homebrew/bin/imsg",
      "dbPath": "/Users/zhangguiping/Library/Messages/chat.db",
      "running": true,
      "lastError": null,
      "probe": {
        "ok": true,
        "privateApi": {
          "available": false,
          "rpcMethods": [
            "chats.list",
            "messages.history",
            "watch.subscribe",
            "watch.unsubscribe"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "channelAccounts": {
    "imessage": [
      {
        "accountId": "default",
        "enabled": true,
        "configured": true,
        "running": true,
        "lastError": null,
        "probe": {
          "ok": true
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       51 passed (51)
Duration    2.82s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The patched PR source starts a real OpenClaw gateway with the real iMessage plugin enabled, not skipped. The gateway starts `/opt/homebrew/bin/imsg` against the local Messages database, reaches `ready`, reports the iMessage account as configured/running with `lastError: null`, and the live channel probe confirms the `watch.subscribe`, `messages.history`, and `chats.list` RPC methods required by the anchorless-payload recovery path. The regression test for the reported live coalescing path still verifies that two anchorless payloads from different groups are repaired before debounce classification, so they no longer share the raw `chat:0` DM bucket.

**Additional live evidence gathered after this patch**:
- A real iMessage group chat exists locally (`chat_id=3`, `is_group=true`), and `imsg messages.history` shows a live URL message in that group:
  - `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84503`
- `imsg watch.subscribe` was started live and returned a real notification for a new group URL message sent with the same account:
  - `subscription=1`
  - `chat_id=3`
  - `is_group=true`
  - `text=https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84503`
- That proves the local `Messages.app` + `imsg` + OpenClaw watch pipeline is live end-to-end on a real group conversation, but the notification payload itself was not malformed.

**What was not tested**: No new malformed group link-preview notification arrived from the local Messages account during this run, so the evidence does not include a live incoming private message/GUID. The live proof above verifies the patched OpenClaw runtime, real `imsg` bridge, local Messages DB access, and iMessage watch-capable channel startup; the exact malformed payload branch is covered by the monitor-level regression test using redacted synthetic GUIDs and handles.

Fixes #84470

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `extensions/imessage/src/monitor.watch-subscribe-retry.test.ts`, `extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts`, `extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts`, `extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Anchorless live iMessage payloads are repaired before inbound debounce classification, unrecoverable anchorless payloads fail closed, and existing conversation-repair/gating/coalescing behavior remains covered.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The regression drives the same monitor/watch path where live `watch.subscribe` notifications enter OpenClaw, while keeping private iMessage handles and GUIDs out of the repository.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Live iMessage watch payloads were entering the inbound debouncer before conversation-anchor recovery. When a malformed group payload looked like `chat_id=0`, empty chat identifiers, and `is_group=false`, `coalesceSameSenderDms` could classify it as a sender DM before the GUID-based repair path had a chance to recover the real group.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The live monitor path lacked a pre-debounce guard for anchorless payloads and lacked regression coverage proving anchorless group payloads cannot be coalesced under a raw DM bucket.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2C Discord migration proof
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the Phase 2C Discord broker routing proof on top of #86137, #86133, and #86096.
- Normalizes broker-routed Discord DMs and channel threads without importing native Discord extension internals.
- Preserves non-channel conversation type in canonical broker routes so Discord DMs remain direct routes.

## Rollout Context

Closes #86111
Related #86108
Related #86105
Stacked on #86137
Stacked on #86133
Stacked on #86096

Native Discord defaults stay unchanged. Existing prefix ownership still keeps `discord:` owned by the native Discord plugin when installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:discord:...` and the broker alias path when native Discord is absent.

For review, the focused stack diff is:
https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and provider-owned ids.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:discord:user:123456789012345678"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command:

```json
{
  "label": "discord-dm",
  "messageId": "native-discord-dm",
  "platform": "discord",
  "conversation": {
    "id": "123456789012345678",
    "type": "direct"
  },
  "requirements": {
    "text": true
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced a direct Discord broker provider request rather than an ambiguous channel-looking target.
- **What was not tested:** Live Discord API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof.

### Behavior

OpenClaw can represent Discord DMs and channel threads through `channel-broker` using stable broker semantics: provider platform `discord`, direct/channel conversation type, conversation id, and optional thread id.

### Environment

- Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`
- Branch: `feat/channel-broker-discord-routing`
- Head: `e5c1b9c685 Add Discord broker routing proof`

### Steps

1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:user:123456789012345678`.
2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "discord"`, `conversation.type: "direct"`, and `conversation.id: "123456789012345678"`.
3. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:channel:222222222222222222?threadId=333333333333333333`.
4. Verified the provider request used channel conversation id plus `threadId`.
5. Resolved the outbound session route for the Discord DM target.
6. Verified the route stayed direct and canonicalized to `discord:direct%3A123456789012345678`.

### Evidence

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts`
  - 2 files passed, 13 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json`
  - passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts`
  - passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix`
  - passed.
- `git diff --check`
  - passed.

### Observed Result

Discord DMs and threads now flow through broker target parsing and provider request semantics, not through core or native Discord extension branches.

### Not Tested

- Live Discord API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector.
- Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions.

## Current Upstream Gate Context

PR #86096 currently has one unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` CI timeout in `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts`; the failed test is outside this broker stack. I attempted to rerun the failed job, but GitHub blocked it because this account does not have repository admin rights.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2D Slack migration proof
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the Phase 2D Slack migration proof for the Channel Broker rollout.
- Keeps native Slack default behavior unchanged; this only proves opt-in broker routing for Slack targets.
- Stacks on Phase 2C Discord proof (#86139), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86137), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86133), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096).

Closes #86112
Related #86108
Related #86105

## What changed

- Normalize broker-prefixed Slack DM targets such as `broker:slack:user:U12345678` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "direct"`.
- Normalize broker-prefixed Slack channel thread targets such as `broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "channel"` and `threadId`.
- Canonicalize Slack broker session routes to the resolved platform target, preserving direct-route semantics as `slack:direct%3A<user>` and thread routes as `slack:<channel>?threadId=<threadTs>`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Slack DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and Slack `threadTs` as broker `threadId`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command:

```json
{
  "label": "slack-thread",
  "messageId": "native-slack-thread",
  "platform": "slack",
  "conversation": {
    "id": "C12345678",
    "type": "channel",
    "threadId": "1716500000.000001"
  },
  "requirements": {
    "text": true,
    "thread": true
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced the expected Slack provider request and kept threaded delivery represented by the broker `threadId` field.
- **What was not tested:** Live Slack API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof.

Focused diff for this stack layer:

https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing

## Validation

Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`:

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix`

## Review state

- Draft until the stacked Channel Broker PR chain clears review.
- Waiting on ClawSweeper/final maintainer review across the stack.
- No native Slack migration is enabled by default in this PR.

target.title: fix(codex): stop persisting per-turn OpenClaw skills snapshot in Codex native user history (#84662)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Codex app-server (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`) wraps the OpenClaw **skills snapshot** into the per-turn user-input text via `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` and `prependCodexOpenClawPromptContext`. Because `turn/start.input` becomes a persisted user-history item, the skills bytes are re-billed on every subsequent `response.create.input`, growing the model input by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause).
- **Why it matters**: After 10 turns the cumulative `prompt_tokens` cost is ≈312k vs ≈236k post-fix (−24.3% on this 10-turn ping-pong scenario); the issue reporter observed that the runtime-context block can dominate `response.create.input` over a longer session.
- **What changed**: Route a **trust-filtered subset of the OpenClaw skills snapshot** through `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` (the same field cron and heartbeat triggers already use in production), via a new `openClawSkillsPrompt` option threaded through `buildTurnStartParams` → `buildTurnCollaborationMode` → `buildTurnScopedCollaborationInstructions`. The fragment is sourced from a new `SkillSnapshot.trustedDeveloperPrompt` field that `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` builds by filtering eligible entries to `source === "openclaw-bundled"` only and running them through the same `compactSkillPaths` / `applySkillsPromptLimits` / `formatSkillsForPrompt` pipeline as the full prompt. `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` now emits the workspace section only and its per-turn wrapper is renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` so the user-input lane is correctly labelled.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: User-editable **workspace context** (incl. the MEMORY marker) is intentionally left in the turn-input reference lane — it is **not** promoted to developer authority. Untrusted skill metadata from workspace, project (`.agents/skills`), personal (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources stays in `SkillSnapshot.prompt` and is **never elevated** to `developer_instructions`. Unit-test invariants pin both (`thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions"; `run-attempt.test.ts` "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions"; `skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources"). Workspace bootstrap (#77372), AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, cron payload guidance, the PI runner `openclaw.runtime-context` path, and the subagent / ACP / CLI surfaces are untouched. **Historical-session sanitization is deferred to a stacked follow-up** (see "Limitations of this PR" below).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations  (codex app-server `turn/start` shape)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Addresses the skills component of #84662. This PR routes future skills snapshots through `developer_instructions` so they are not appended to Codex native user history going forward. It does **not** implement the issue's *safe interim* historical sanitizer for already-bound threads; that half is deferred to a stacked follow-up (see "Limitations of this PR").
- Related: supersedes closed #84790 (TurboTheTurtle, closed 2026-05-22) by narrowing scope to the skills snapshot only — workspace/MEMORY stays in the turn-input reference lane that #84790's [P1] prompt-authority finding was raised against.
- Related: complements merged #83454 (Eva, 2026-05-18) — skills are treated as **stable OpenClaw plugin/tool guidance** and routed through `developer_instructions`, while user-editable workspace text **remains user-reference scoped in turn input** per #83454. This is not a reversal of #83454: the user-editable surface that #83454 protected is left where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned plugin-manifest surface moves, joining the workspace bootstrap files (#77372) that already live in `developer_instructions`.
- Related: builds on merged #77372 (lonexreb, workspace bootstrap → `developer_instructions`) as the lane-precedent for OpenClaw-owned stable instructions.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: The shared per-turn wrapper `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` conflates two fragments with different authority + lifetime needs:
  1. **Skills snapshot** (`params.skillsSnapshot?.prompt`) — a mixed-source catalog. OpenClaw-owned bundled skills (`openclaw-bundled`) are byte-stable, OpenClaw-controlled plugin manifest text, analogous to the workspace bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) that already live in `developer_instructions` via #77372. The same field also carries SKILL.md frontmatter from workspace, project (`.agents/skills`), personal (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources whose content is user/install-controlled.
  2. **Workspace prompt context** (`workspaceBootstrapContext.promptContext`) — **user-editable** workspace files (e.g., `MEMORY.md`, custom project docs). Per #84662 §Security/rank caution, this must remain quoted/reference scoped.
  Bundling both into `turn/start.input[0].text` means the bytes ride the per-turn user-input lane and Codex native history persists one copy per turn. Issue #84662 carves these two halves apart explicitly; the wider scope from #84790 collapsed them back together and ran into the prompt-authority blocker raised in that review.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No unit test pinned the authority boundary (workspace user-editable text must not appear in `developer_instructions`, and untrusted-source skill metadata must not appear in `developer_instructions`). This PR adds three: `thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions", `run-attempt.test.ts` "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions", and `skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources".
- **Contributing context**: cron and heartbeat triggers already populate `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` in production. The generated `@openai/codex` 0.132.0 `v2/TurnStartParams.ts` does not currently declare `collaborationMode` (the formal basis for #84790's [P1] protocol concern), but the bundled runtime binary parses and honors the field on cron / heartbeat triggers today — empirical evidence below shows it also honors it on the chat trigger.

## Security boundary

Trust-filter policy for the developer-instructions lane (`turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`):

- **Allowed (elevated)**: skill entries whose `resolveSkillSource(entry.skill) === "openclaw-bundled"`. Only the OpenClaw-bundled catalog ships shipped/reviewed SKILL.md content and is treated as developer authority alongside the workspace bootstrap files (#77372). The trusted fragment is built by `buildTrustedDeveloperSkillsPrompt` in `src/agents/skills/workspace.ts` from the same `promptEntries` set that drives `SkillSnapshot.prompt`, sorted, char-budgeted, and rendered via `formatSkillsForPrompt` / `formatSkillsCompact` — the byte shape is identical to the full prompt, just with the untrusted entries removed.
- **Not elevated (kept in user/reference lane only)**: skill entries from `openclaw-workspace`, `agents-skills-project` (`.agents/skills`), `agents-skills-personal` (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, plugin-generated, and any source not on the bundled allowlist. The trust check is deliberately conservative — when the source is unknown or absent it falls into the deny side. These entries remain visible to the model through `SkillSnapshot.prompt`, which continues to ride the existing `commands-system-prompt.ts` / pi-side surfaces (untouched by this PR); they are simply not granted Codex developer-instruction authority.
- **Wire-level invariants pinned by regression tests**:
  - `developer_instructions` contains only trust-filtered bundled skill descriptions/locations, even when `skillsSnapshot.prompt` carries the full mixed catalog (`run-attempt.test.ts`: "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions").
  - `developer_instructions` falls back to the base collaboration preset (no `## OpenClaw Skills` section) when `trustedDeveloperPrompt` is undefined, e.g. workspace-only catalogs without any bundled skill (`run-attempt.test.ts`: "omits skills from developer_instructions when no trusted skills are present").
  - The trusted-developer prompt fragment built by `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` includes the bundled skill's description/location but excludes every untrusted source's name, description, and location (`skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts`: "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources").
  - `trustedDeveloperPrompt` is `undefined` (not an empty string, not a stripped catalog) when no trusted skills are present, so the developer-instructions lane null-falls back cleanly (`skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts`: "omits trustedDeveloperPrompt when no bundled skills are present").
  - Provider-boundary precheck, `llm_input` observer, trajectory `context.compiled.systemPrompt`, and `result.systemPromptReport.skills.{promptChars,entries}` all reflect the trusted-only bytes — not the full mixed catalog — so operators see the actual developer-lane size.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts` (6 cases for cron / heartbeat / chat / authority invariant), `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (existing "routes OpenClaw skills through turn collaboration developer_instructions" updated to use `trustedDeveloperPrompt`; **2 new cases** — `does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions`, `omits skills from developer_instructions when no trusted skills are present`), `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts` (1 context-engine budget assertion updated to use `trustedDeveloperPrompt`), `src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` (**2 new cases** — bundled-only trust filter; trusted prompt is `undefined` when no bundled skills present), plus the prompt-snapshot helper/check (`test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts`, `test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts`).
- Scenario the test should lock in: (a) chat trigger renders skills as `developer_instructions`; (b) chat trigger without skills returns `null` `developer_instructions`; (c) cron composes cron + skills; (d) heartbeat composes heartbeat + overlay + skills; (e) cron without skills is byte-identical to current main; (f) workspace user-editable context never reaches `developer_instructions` (authority invariant); (g) untrusted skill metadata (workspace, project, personal, managed, extra) never reaches `developer_instructions`, even when `skillsSnapshot.prompt` carries it (prompt-authority boundary); (h) trusted-developer prompt fragment from `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` is bundled-only; (i) context-engine / prompt-snapshot surfaces report the workspace-only turn-input projection rather than the old runtime-context wrapper.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is structural (wrong lane plus wrong trust scope); the structural invariants pin all three halves (skills present in `developer_instructions`, workspace absent from it, untrusted skill metadata absent from it) without needing live-model evidence to rerun on every CI tick.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- **Per-turn token billing on the default `main` agent** (new turns after this PR ships): skills bytes no longer re-bill in every `response.create.input`; the cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` cost drops by ~24% on the ping-pong scenario in the Real-behavior proof. Workspace-context bytes continue to re-bill at the same rate as before (~+540/turn floor).
- **Per-turn user-input wrapper text** (new turns): renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:\n…` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:\n…`. The block now contains only the `## OpenClaw Workspace Context` section; the `## OpenClaw Skills` section is gone (moved to `developer_instructions`).
- **Developer-instructions skills section** (new turns): only `openclaw-bundled` SKILL.md descriptions/locations appear here. SKILL.md from workspace, project, personal, `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources remains discoverable via `skillsSnapshot.prompt` on the user/reference lane (untouched in this PR), but never appears in the elevated developer-instructions lane.
- **Existing Codex native threads bound before this PR ships**: see "Limitations of this PR" below.
- **Snapshot fixtures**: three `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md` files regenerated to reflect the new workspace-context wrapper text and `userInputText.chars` (≈ +15 chars from the wording change). These fixtures cover the deterministic happy-path projection; the live proof plus the new unit/context-engine tests are the guard that the real 6,557-char skills section no longer sits in `turn/start.input`.

## Limitations of this PR (deferred half explicitly disclosed)

This PR **prevents future skills bytes from being appended** to Codex native user history. It does **not** sanitize history for threads that were bound before the fix ships.

- **Existing bound Codex native threads** that already contain `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items from prior turns will continue to replay those items on `response.create.input` until either (a) the thread cycles to a fresh native session, or (b) the deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer (issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix") lands as a stacked follow-up PR that strips the wrapper from `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages`.
- The snapshot-fixture check (`workspaceBootstrapContextFrom` field in `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md`) verifies the **current projection shape** for new turns; it is **not** a regression guard for the deferred sanitizer half — old mirrored-history user items are out of its scope.
- The 10-turn benchmark below was run with fresh user IDs (i.e., fresh Codex native sessions), which is why the patched arm's per-turn `prompt_tokens` curve flattens immediately. Users on long-lived bound threads will see the curve flatten only after the next thread reset, or after the follow-up sanitizer lands.

The deferred follow-up will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle` (PR #84790's sanitizer logic) and is independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.

## Authority safety (why this is not the #84790 design)

| | PR #84790 (CLOSED 2026-05-22) | This PR |
|---|---|---|
| Content moved into `developer_instructions` | skills + workspace + MEMORY | **trust-filtered bundled skills only** |
| Skill source policy | full mixed-source catalog (bundled + workspace + project + personal + managed + extra + plugin) elevated | **`openclaw-bundled` only**; workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin SKILL.md stays in user/reference lane |
| Workspace / MEMORY routing | through `developer_instructions` (with a disclaimer wrapper) | **unchanged from current `main`** — stays in turn-input reference lane established by #83454 |
| Session-history sanitization | bundled in same PR | **deferred follow-up** (disclosed above) |
| Protocol-acceptance argument | schema inspection only | schema + production cron / heartbeat parity + wire-level Codex native rollout + cacheRead ratio uplift on the same scenario |
| Authority invariant test | n/a | three unit tests pin both halves: workspace text never appears in `developer_instructions`, and untrusted skill metadata never appears in `developer_instructions` |

Reading order:

- Issue #84662 §"Security/rank caution": *"Stable instructions such as OpenClaw skills may be safe to move to developer/bootstrap instructions, but user-editable workspace context should remain quoted/reference scoped."* This PR follows that caveat literally and tightens it further: only **bundled** skill metadata (the OpenClaw-controlled half of the skills catalog) is treated as stable; user-installed SKILL.md content from workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin sources stays in the reference lane.
- PR #84790 was closed by its author after the wider scope failed the prompt-authority finding raised in that review (wrapper text does not actually downgrade message authority).
- PR #83454 deliberately moved both halves *out* of `developer_instructions` so user-editable text would not gain developer authority. This PR complements that policy: workspace text stays where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned bundled plugin-manifest skills snapshot — a stable, OpenClaw-controlled surface analogous to the bootstrap files in #77372 — moves to `developer_instructions`.
- PR #77372 already routes workspace-bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) through `developer_instructions`; bundled skills join their natural peers under this PR.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Codex app-server persists the OpenClaw skills snapshot as a per-turn user-history item in native Codex threads, causing the model's `response.create.input` to grow by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause). After this fix, the trust-filtered bundled-skills fragment rides `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` as turn-scoped developer guidance instead of being appended to native user history; the payload is byte-stable/cacheable across new turns and is not replayed from user-history for **new turns on threads opened after this PR ships**. The 10-turn live benchmark below was captured at the prior HEAD (`66ee52173e`) where the developer-instructions lane carried the full skills catalog. The follow-up commit (`646691d62b`) restricts that lane to `openclaw-bundled` entries only; this is a **strict reduction** of what is sent on the developer lane (fewer bytes, identical wire shape), so the cacheable / byte-stable / no-replay properties continue to hold and the token-growth direction does not regress. The byte sizes below now reflect bundled-only entries rather than the full mixed catalog.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw checkout at the upstream/main HEAD this PR is based on, rebuilt dist; live OpenAI Codex backend (`openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`); two local OpenAI-compatible OpenClaw gateways on separate ports sharing the same eval state directory (one unpatched baseline, one with this PR's patch applied); a live 10-turn ping-pong benchmark as the proof surface. A separate live runtime instance on an unrelated port was observed before / during / after the experiment via `/healthz` to confirm no PID change in unrelated processes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
- Start two OpenClaw gateways on separate ports against an eval `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, one built from current upstream/main (arm A — baseline) and one built from this PR's branch (used for arms A' and B), each launched via `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway` with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`.
- Drive a 10-turn `body_contains: "pong"` ping-pong scenario against each gateway via an OpenAI-compatible benchmark CLI, using a distinct user ID per arm (forces a fresh Codex native session per arm) for three arms:
  - Arm A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state), default `main` agent.
  - Arm A' — patched, default `main` agent.
  - Arm B — patched gateway, but a separately-configured agent with `tools.profile=minimal`, `systemPromptOverride="."`, `skills=[]` (i.e., a no-skills control).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/{run-attempt,thread-lifecycle,run-attempt.context-engine}.test.ts --run`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts --run`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts --run`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

**Evidence after fix (10-turn live benchmark × 3 arms, all 30/30 functional `body_contains: "pong"`)**:

| Arm | growth/turn | turn 1 / turn 10 prompt_tokens | cum 10t prompt_tokens | cacheRead/input (turn 9 steady) | uncached input tokens (turn 9) | skills.count / promptChars |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state) | +2,194 | 21,286 / 41,032 | 311,590 | 93.27 % | 2,614 | 20 / 6,629 |
| A' — patched | **+535** | 21,166 / 25,981 | **235,735** | **96.08 %** | **998** | 20 / 6,557 |
| B — no-skills control (patched gateway, no-skills agent) | +540 | 19,552 / 24,412 | 219,820 | 95.98 % | 960 | 0 / 0 |

All three arms used **fresh** user IDs so each arm opened a new Codex native session — the patched-arm curve reflects the new-thread behavior, not the legacy-thread replay floor. Per the "Limitations" section above, legacy bound threads are out of scope for this proof.

- Per-turn growth on the default agent drops from +2,194 to +535 tokens/turn (**−75.6 %**); cumulative 10-turn save = **−75,855 tokens (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline.
- Arm B's no-skills floor (+540/turn) is within ±5 tokens/turn of arm A' — confirming the +535/turn residual on the patched arm is the workspace-context replay floor, **not** skills. This PR intentionally leaves that floor in place per #84662 §Security/rank caution; reducing it requires either the deferred history sanitizer or a separate workspace-routing decision (out of scope).
- Codex native rollout for the patched arm shows `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` populated with a byte-stable payload (the 6,740-char number above includes 20 mixed-source skill entries from the prior HEAD; the new HEAD reduces this to the bundled subset, which is a strict size reduction) starting `OpenClaw skills available for this turn:\nThese skills describe OpenClaw plugin capabilities…\n\n## OpenClaw Skills\n…`. Byte-stability is the precondition for the OpenAI prefix cache; the patched arm reaches the same **96.08 %** steady-state cacheRead at turn 9 as the no-skills control (95.98 %), a **+2.81 pp** uplift over baseline at the same turn index. The smaller bundled-only payload preserves byte-stability (the bundled catalog is the most stable subset across rebuilds) and therefore preserves the cacheRead uplift; it can only improve it.
- Workspace context bytes (including the MEMORY marker) appear only in the per-turn user message under the renamed wrapper `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` and never in `developer_instructions` — verified both at the wire level (Codex native rollout) and as a unit-test structural invariant. Untrusted-source SKILL.md content (workspace, project, personal, managed, extra, plugin) likewise never appears in `developer_instructions` — verified by the new `does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions` unit test and by `trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources`.

**Latency**: trimmed mean **−448 ms (−8.8 %)** on the patched arm vs the baseline arm (5,102 → 4,654 ms after dropping hi/lo across n=10), both arms dominated by a single turn-1 cold-start outlier (A: 26,862 ms, A': 22,453 ms). No functional regression. We do **not** claim latency improvement at n=10; a 50-turn rerun would be the right place to make a stronger latency claim.

**Observed result after fix**: per-turn `prompt_tokens` growth on the default `main` agent drops from **+2,194 → +535 tokens/turn** (−75.6 %), within ±5 tokens/turn of the no-skills control (+540). Cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` saves **−75,855 (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline. 30 / 30 functional `body_contains: "pong"` success across the 3 arms. `developer_instructions` is byte-stable across all 10 turns on the patched arm; workspace / MEMORY text never appears in `developer_instructions` (verified at the wire level and as a unit-test structural invariant), and untrusted-source SKILL.md content never appears in `developer_instructions` (verified by unit tests on the new trust filter). No functional regression observed.

**What was not tested**:

- A re-run of the 10-turn live benchmark against the post-trust-filter content commit (`646691d62b`). The change between the prior benchmarked HEAD (`66ee52173e`) and `646691d62b` is a strict reduction of bytes on the developer lane (filter to bundled-only); it does not change the wire shape, the byte-stability across turns, or the no-history-replay property the proof established. The unit tests above pin the structural invariants the benchmark would re-establish. Current HEAD `db5a342d98d39a4ed34e6dc6ea6d405094547ee6` is an empty CI-retrigger commit on top of `646691d62b`, with no source or test diff vs `646691d62b`, so the same reasoning applies to the live HEAD.
- Long-session 50 / 100-turn scaling under this change (deferred to a follow-up long-session validation; the +535/turn residual rate predicts a ~107k cumulative save at 50 turns versus the unpatched curve, but this is unmeasured here).
- Workspace-file mid-session edit cache-bust behaviour (workspace was static across the ~30 s experiment).
- Out-of-sample models (only `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini` was exercised).
- Live Discord / Telegram production-channel turn (the OpenAI-compatible gateway was the proof surface, not a channel).
- **Legacy bound Codex native threads** that contain pre-fix `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items — by design (see "Limitations of this PR"). The deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer is what makes those threads stop replaying historical wrappers.
- Session-history sanitization itself, including the `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing path — out of scope for this PR (deferred to stacked follow-up).
- `skills.promptChars` drifted by 72 chars between the two arms (6,629 vs 6,557) due to a bundled-plugin asset rebuild difference between the two checkouts, not the source patch; `skills.entries.count` is **20 / 20** in both arms and the same skill names appear in both arms' Codex native rollouts.
- A single turn-10 OpenAI cache-eviction anomaly on the patched arm reported `cached_input_tokens=10,624` (turn-1-like) and `uncached=15,357` against the byte-stable `developer_instructions` payload (md5 stable); turns 2–9 each showed ~96.1 % cache rate and the steady-state quoted above uses turn 9. The headline per-turn growth and cumulative `prompt_tokens` metrics are unaffected (they count both cached and uncached input). A repeat run 12 hours earlier on a prior upstream/main base reported 96.07 % at the same turn-10 index, so the turn-10 dip is a one-off platform-side event, not a behavior of the patch.

## Validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts --run
node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
pnpm check:changed
```

Local results captured at content HEAD `646691d62b668daaeec350fd2c4973a2e9956d47` (the trust-filter commit; `db5a342d98` is an empty CI-retrigger commit on top of it with no source or test diff, so these results carry forward unchanged):

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/{thread-lifecycle,run-attempt,run-attempt.context-engine}.test.ts` — 280/280 tests passed.
- `src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` — 11/11 tests passed (including the 2 new trust-filter cases).
- `test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts` — 10/10 tests passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` — Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
- `pnpm check:changed` — exit 0 (typecheck, lint, import cycles, shrinkwrap, package patch, runtime sidecar loader guards, media download helper guard all green).

All required CI workflows are green at current HEAD `db5a342d98d39a4ed34e6dc6ea6d405094547ee6` (run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26367735445).

## Follow-up planned (separate stacked PR)

Session-history sanitizer for already-bound Codex native threads that already carry legacy `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items. Implements `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing per issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix". Builds on the sanitizer logic PR #84790 contributed; will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle`. Independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.
target.author: masatohoshino

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: fix(cli): route node status hints to stdout
target.body:
## Summary

- route `openclaw node status` recovery hints for missing-unit and stopped-runtime states through stdout
- keep the primary status/error summary on stderr so scripts can still detect the failure state
- add focused coverage for both affected node status branches

Fixes #83925.

## Verification

2026-05-24 update after rebasing onto current `main`, head `2fd3cd10ca`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts`
- `git diff --check main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw node status` printed follow-up recovery hints to stderr for loaded-but-stopped and missing service-unit states, unlike the unloaded branch which already printed hints on stdout.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04, source checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-85780`, branch `fix-83925-node-status-hints`, head `2fd3cd10ca`.

Proof setup: created a temporary unstarted systemd user node service from the patched checkout, using a disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME` and a temporary untracked `dist/entry.js` stub only for service installation. The service was uninstalled immediately after the status capture, and cleanup confirmed `openclaw-node.service` was `not-found` afterward.

Redacted command shape:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node install --force
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node stop
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node status >status.stdout 2>status.stderr
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node uninstall
systemctl --user is-enabled openclaw-node.service
```

Redacted output after fix:

```text
--- status stdout ---
Service: systemd user (enabled)
Command: /usr/bin/node /REDACTED/openclaw-85780/dist/entry.js node run --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18789
Service file: ~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-node.service
Runtime: stopped (state inactive, sub dead, last exit 0, reason 1)
Logs: journalctl --user -u openclaw-node.service -n 200 --no-pager
Restart attempts: ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway-restart.log
--- status stderr ---
Service is loaded but not running.
--- cleanup check ---
not-found
stub_removed=yes
```

Observed result after fix: the stopped-service recovery hints (`Logs:` and `Restart attempts:`) were emitted on stdout, while the primary failure summary stayed on stderr.

## Summary
- enable Codex app-server native code mode when runtime toolsAllow explicitly includes the full native surface: exec/read/write/edit
- keep dynamic-only and partial-native allowlists fail-closed
- preserve current memory-flush, node exec host, and sandbox policy gates on main

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "native tool surfaces"` (6 passed after rebase)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Earlier before rebase: `pytest tests/test_review_wake.py` in `projects/jay-operating-system` passed 18 tests.
- Earlier before rebase: `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review` completed with no accepted/actionable findings after accepted findings were fixed.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Daily OS Codex cron wakes can request the native tool surface through `toolsAllow=exec,read,write,edit,message,memory_search,memory_get`; the Codex app-server should enable native code mode for that full native allowlist instead of dispatching a dynamic-tool-only turn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout used by the production Telegram agent host at `/root/.openclaw/openclaw-src-v5.19`, with the real Daily OS review wake job inspected at `/root/.openclaw/workspace/projects/jay-operating-system/src/jay_operating_system/review_wake.py`.

Evidence after fix: Console output from the post-rebase local source checkout:

```text
$ git log --oneline -3 --decorate
```

target.title: fix(mattermost): route thread replies correctly when replyToMode is off
target.body:
> **Depends on #57970** (`fix/mattermost-unknown-chat-type`). This branch is rebased on that PR. After #57970 merges, this PR should be rebased onto `main`.

With `replyToMode: "off"`, `resolveMattermostEffectiveReplyToId` returns `undefined`
to prevent thread session forking. However, this also broke two delivery behaviours
that depended on `effectiveReplyToId` being set:

1. `MessageThreadId` was always `undefined` in the inbound context, so tools and
   plugin hooks had no way to know which thread a message came from.

2. Agent replies went to the main channel instead of the originating thread, because
   all three deliver call sites passed `effectiveReplyToId` (undefined) as the
   `threadRootId` to `resolveMattermostReplyRootId`. Plugin command responses share
   the same deliver path and are affected by the same bug.

Fix:
- `resolveMattermostThreadSessionContext` now computes `messageThreadId` separately
  as `post.root_id ?? effectiveReplyToId`, reflecting the actual thread root
  regardless of `replyToMode`.
- `MessageThreadId` in the inbound `ctxPayload` is now set from `messageThreadId`
  at all three dispatch sites (interaction, model-picker/sendMessage, main handler).
- All three `resolveMattermostReplyRootId` deliver call sites now pass
  `messageThreadId` as `threadRootId` instead of `effectiveReplyToId`, so replies
  are delivered to the correct thread.
- All three `sendTypingIndicator` call sites now pass `messageThreadId` so typing
  indicators appear in the same thread as the eventual reply.
- Added `buildToolContext` to the Mattermost threading config so that
  `currentThreadTs` is populated from `MessageThreadId`, giving agent tools
  visibility into the active thread.
- Fixed `resolveThreadFlag` in `src/config/sessions/reset.ts` to not classify a
  session as thread-scoped when `messageThreadId` is set but the session key
  explicitly identifies a group/channel session. `messageThreadId` is a delivery
  detail; the session key is authoritative for session type.
- Removed the `channelTypeKnown` workaround from `handlePost` — now dead code
  because the `kind === "unknown"` early-exit guard from #57970 prevents reaching
  the threading logic with an unresolved channel type.

## Summary

- **Problem:** With `replyToMode: "off"`, any message posted inside a Mattermost
  thread caused `effectiveReplyToId` to be `undefined`. All three deliver call sites
  used `effectiveReplyToId` as the thread root for outgoing replies, so agent replies
  posted to the main channel instead of the originating thread. Plugin command
  responses share the same deliver path and are affected identically. Additionally,
  `ctx.MessageThreadId` was always `undefined`, so tools had no visibility into the
  active thread.
- **Why it matters:** Users with `replyToMode: "off"` (the typical setup for bots
  that should not fork per-thread sessions) could not use Mattermost threads at all
  — every agent reply landed in the main channel instead of the thread it came from.
- **What changed:** `resolveMattermostThreadSessionContext` now returns a separate
  `messageThreadId` field (`post.root_id ?? effectiveReplyToId`) that carries the
  actual thread root independent of `replyToMode`. All three deliver call sites,
  all three typing indicator call sites, and all three `ctxPayload` construction
  sites now use `messageThreadId` instead of `effectiveReplyToId`.
  `buildToolContext` added to the Mattermost threading config so `currentThreadTs`
  is populated. `resolveThreadFlag` updated to treat `messageThreadId` as a
  delivery detail rather than a session-type signal when the session key is
  explicitly a group/channel session. Removed the `channelTypeKnown` workaround
  (dead code after #57970).
- **What did NOT change:** Session routing is unaffected — `effectiveReplyToId` and
  `resolveThreadSessionKeys` are unchanged, so `replyToMode: "off"` still prevents
  thread session forking exactly as intended.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Depends on #57970 (`fix/mattermost-unknown-chat-type`)
- Related #29587 (feat: add replyToMode support)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `effectiveReplyToId` was serving two distinct purposes — session
  routing (which session key to use) and delivery routing (where to post the reply).
  Commit `aaba1ae653` correctly set it to `undefined` for session routing when
  `replyToMode: "off"`, but inadvertently broke delivery routing as a side effect.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** Tests for `resolveMattermostThreadSessionContext`
  only asserted on `effectiveReplyToId` and `sessionKey`. No test covered the
  end-to-end delivery path (deliver callback → `resolveMattermostReplyRootId` →
  actual post target) for the `replyToMode: "off"` + threaded message scenario.
- **Prior context:** `replyToMode` support was added in 2026.3.12 (`171d2df9e0`,
  PR #29587). The regression was introduced in 2026.3.22 by `aaba1ae653`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts`,
  `resolveMattermostThreadSessionContext` describe block
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** When `replyToMode: "off"` and a non-zero
  `threadRootId` is present, `messageThreadId` equals `threadRootId` and
  `effectiveReplyToId` is still `undefined`.
- **Existing test that already covers this:** Added
  `"still reports messageThreadId when replyToMode is off"` in this PR; also updated
  the existing `"keeps threaded messages top-level when replyToMode is off"`
  assertion to include `messageThreadId: "root-456"`.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

With `replyToMode: "off"`, agent replies now go to the originating thread instead
of the main channel. Typing indicators appear in the same thread. Plugin command
responses are also fixed as they share the same delivery path. No change in
behaviour for `replyToMode: "all"` or `"first"`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before (replyToMode: "off", user posts inside thread T1):
  post.root_id="T1"
    -> effectiveReplyToId=undefined
    -> resolveMattermostReplyRootId(threadRootId=undefined, replyToId=undefined)
    -> reply posts to main channel  ✗

After:
  post.root_id="T1"
    -> messageThreadId="T1"   effectiveReplyToId=undefined (unchanged)
    -> resolveMattermostReplyRootId(threadRootId="T1")
    -> reply posts to thread T1  ✓
    -> session key unchanged (no forking)  ✓
    -> resolveThreadFlag: session key is ":channel:" → isThread=false  ✓
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Integration/channel: Mattermost, account with `replyToMode: "off"`
- Relevant config: `channels.mattermost.replyToMode: "off"` (or per-account equivalent)

### Steps

1. Configure a Mattermost account with `replyToMode: "off"`
2. Post a message inside a channel thread
3. Observe where the agent reply appears

### Expected

- Agent reply appears inside the thread

### Actual (before fix)

- Agent reply appears in the main channel

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on unpatched 2026.3.28: with `replyToMode: "off"`, agent replies land in
  the main channel instead of the originating thread. Confirmed fixed with this PR.
- Typing indicators also appear in-thread after fix.
- Edge cases checked: top-level messages with `replyToMode: "off"` unaffected;
  `replyToMode: "all"` behaviour unchanged.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `messageThreadId` used where `effectiveReplyToId` was previously `undefined`
  for `replyToMode: "all"/"first"` top-level posts — could change delivery target.
  - Mitigation: `messageThreadId = threadRootId ?? effectiveReplyToId`, so for
    non-`"off"` modes the value is identical to the old `effectiveReplyToId`. No
    behaviour change for those modes.
- Risk: setting `MessageThreadId` could misclassify channel sessions as thread
  sessions in `resolveThreadFlag`, changing reset policy for users with separate
  `session.resetByType.thread` config.
  - Mitigation: fixed in `src/config/sessions/reset.ts` — `resolveThreadFlag` now
    defers to the session key when it explicitly identifies a group/channel session,
    treating `messageThreadId` as a delivery detail rather than a session-type signal.

target.title: fix(sessions): let a2a policy gate cross-agent sends independently of visibility
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #57447

- `createSessionVisibilityGuard()` blocked all cross-agent access when `visibility !== "all"`, including `sessions_send`, even when `agentToAgent` policy explicitly allowed it
- This forced an all-or-nothing choice: `visibility=all` exposes all sessions, `visibility=tree` blocks legitimate cross-agent messaging
- One-line fix: skip the visibility gate for `"send"` action on cross-agent targets, letting a2a policy be the sole authorization gate

## Rationale

| Action | Gate | Rationale |
|--------|------|-----------|
| list | visibility | controls what you can **see** |
| history | visibility | controls what you can **read** |
| status | visibility | controls what you can **inspect** |
| send | a2a policy | controls what you can **message** — sending does not require seeing |

## Changes

- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts`: Add `params.action !== "send"` to the cross-agent visibility check
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts`: Add 2 tests — send allowed with tree+a2a, history still blocked with tree+a2a

## Test plan

- [x] 14 unit tests pass (`sessions-access.test.ts`)
- [ ] Manual: configure `visibility=tree` + `agentToAgent.enabled=true`, verify cross-agent `sessions_send` works while `sessions_history` remains blocked

target.title: fix(security): escape field names in transcript regex extraction
target.body:
## Summary

`extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` and `extractJsonNullableStringFieldPrefix` in `session-utils.fs.ts` interpolate the `field` parameter directly into `new RegExp(...)` without escaping. All current callers pass hardcoded field names (`"id"`, `"parentId"`, `"type"`, `"role"`), but the function signature accepts any `string`. A future caller passing a field containing regex metacharacters (e.g. `"foo.bar"`) would match unintended patterns (`.` matches any character instead of a literal dot).

This wraps the interpolation with `escapeRegExp()` from `src/shared/regexp.ts` so metacharacters are treated literally.

## Verification

219 session-utils tests pass (gateway-core, gateway-server, gateway-client). The new `oversized line metadata extraction preserves id and parentId` test exercises the escapeRegExp-guarded regex path.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Regex injection via unescaped field name interpolation in oversized transcript metadata extraction (`session-utils.fs.ts:276,293`). Metacharacter field names cause extraction failure or wrong-value extraction.

**Real environment tested:** macOS arm64, Node 26.0.0, openclaw checkout at patched head.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Standalone runtime script that replicates the vulnerable and fixed extraction functions, then runs each against JSON prefixes containing field names with regex metacharacters:

```
node --import tsx /tmp/proof-85934.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== PR #85934: field name regex escape proof ===

Field with parentheses: count(items)
  Input: {"count(items)":"5","other":"x"}
  Before fix: EXTRACTION FAILED
  After fix:  5

Field with brackets: tags[0]
  Input: {"tags[0]":"first","tags0x":"other"}
  Before fix: EXTRACTION FAILED
  After fix:  first

Field with plus: rate+tax
  Input: {"rate+tax":"15","ratetax":"10"}
  Before fix: 10
  After fix:  15

Field with $: $ref
  Input: {"$ref":"#/definitions/model","ref":"fallback"}
  Before fix: EXTRACTION FAILED
  After fix:  #/definitions/model

Safe field name (normal transcript): id
  Input: {"type":"message","id":"msg-abc123","parentId":"root"}
  Before fix: msg-abc123
  After fix:  msg-abc123
  Both work correctly for safe field names.

escapeRegExp neutralizes metacharacters while safe names are unaffected.
```

Without escapeRegExp, parentheses, brackets, and dollar signs cause extraction to fail entirely, and the plus sign causes extraction of the wrong value (`10` from `"ratetax"` instead of `15` from `"rate+tax"`). With escapeRegExp, all metacharacter field names are treated literally and extract the correct value.

**Observed result after fix:** The escapeRegExp wrapper fixes four classes of metacharacter failures (parentheses, brackets, plus, dollar sign) while leaving safe field names unaffected. The plus-sign case is the most dangerous: it silently returns the wrong value instead of failing.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end production transcript pipeline with adversarial field names (all current callers use hardcoded safe strings). The fix is defense-in-depth.

target.title: feat(context-pruning): cache media to disk during pruning instead of stripping
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Context pruning removes image/media blocks and replaces them with `[image removed during context pruning]`, losing media permanently from the session
- Why it matters: Agents lose access to previously-shared images mid-conversation, degrading multi-turn visual reasoning
- What changed: When `cacheMedia: true` is configured, pruned media is saved to disk (`~/.openclaw/media/cache/`) and replaced with `[media cached: <path>]` markers that agents can read back on demand
- What did NOT change: Default behavior (`cacheMedia: false`) is unchanged — existing pruning logic preserved

### Bug fixes included (post-review)

| # | File | Bug | Fix |
|---|------|-----|-----|
| 1 | `pruner.ts` | soft-trim + hard-clear: `PRUNED_CONTEXT_IMAGE_MARKER` text was discarded when a message went through both phases, breaking `writePrunedMediaCaches` | Replaced `softTrimmedIndexes` Set with `softPhaseMediaCount` Map; hard-clear re-injects the right number of placeholder markers |
| 2 | `extension.ts` | multi-image hard-clear: `writePrunedMediaCaches` restarted the full `refs` loop for every content block, so block N got ref 0's cached path instead of ref N's | Changed to a single `refIdx` counter advancing sequentially across all blocks |
| 3 | `media-cache.ts` | TOCTOU race: `access()` then `writeFile()` with concurrent `Promise.allSettled` writes — a racing caller could observe a partially-written file | Write to `${hash}.pid.tmp` then `rename()` atomically; concurrent writers landing first are handled gracefully |
| 4 | `media-cache.ts` | MIME normalization: `mediaCacheKind` and `buildCachedMediaMarker` passed raw MIME to `mediaKindFromMime`, so `image/png; charset=binary` or `IMAGE/PNG` → `kind=document` → `images.ts` silently dropped the cached image | Both functions now call `normalizeMimeType()` before classification/embedding |
| 5 | `pruner.ts` | `extractCachedMediaMarkers` preserved entire text blocks (including soft-trim head/tail and trim notes) into hard-cleared output, defeating the clear | Now extracts only the `[media cached: ...]` marker strings via regex, not the surrounding block content |

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Supersedes #53869 (closed for consolidation)

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A — new feature with post-review bug fixes

## Security Checklist

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Steps

1. Set `agents.defaults.contextPruning.cacheMedia: true`
2. Send images to agent in a long conversation
3. When context pruning triggers, verify images cached to `~/.openclaw/media/cache/`
4. Verify `[media cached: ...]` markers appear in context

## Evidence

- [x] Unit tests: `src/media/media-cache.test.ts` covers write, dedup, path assertions
- [x] Unit tests: `src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.test.ts` — 5 new tests covering soft-trim, hard-clear-only, soft-trim+hard-clear (bug path), and multi-image collection
- [x] Cross-platform: path.sep assertions for Windows compatibility

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: all commits rebased on origin/main, all checks passing
- Edge cases covered: paths with spaces, hard-clear-only media refs, soft-trim+hard-clear interaction, multi-image messages, non-normalized MIME types, concurrent duplicate writes
- Not verified: live multi-turn session (needs integration test environment)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — opt-in via config
- Config/env changes? New optional config key `agents.defaults.contextPruning.cacheMedia`
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Disk usage from cached media accumulates
  - Mitigation: TTL-based cleanup in media-cache module

target.body:
## Summary

Expose the `knownLongFlags` field in the `safeBinProfiles` config schema, allowing operators to declare long flags as "known" for GNU abbreviation resolution without misusing `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`.

Closes #56805

## Problem

The `knownLongFlags` field exists internally in `SafeBinProfile` and is used by `validateSafeBinArgv` for GNU long-option abbreviation resolution. However, it is not present in:

1. The Zod schema (`ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema`) — `.strict()` causes an "Unrecognized key" crash
2. The `SafeBinProfileFixture` type — no way to pass it through config normalization

For empty profiles `{}`, `knownLongFlags` auto-derives as `[]`, causing **all** long flags to be treated as unknown and rejected. The only workaround is to list every possible long flag in `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`, which changes their semantics.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags: z.array(z.string()).optional()` to `ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema` |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy-profiles.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to `SafeBinProfileFixture` type; update `normalizeSafeBinProfileFixture` and `compileSafeBinProfile` to normalize and merge with auto-derived flags |
| `src/config/normalize-exec-safe-bin.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to the type cast in normalization |
| `src/config/schema.base.generated.ts` | Regenerated via `generate-base-config-schema.ts` |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` | 6 new tests for the config path |

## Design Decisions

- **Merge, don't replace**: User-provided `knownLongFlags` are merged with auto-derived flags from `allowedValueFlags ∪ deniedFlags`, ensuring flags in those lists are always "known" even if not explicitly listed
- **Reuses `normalizeFixtureFlags`**: Same normalization (trim, dedupe, sort) applied to `knownLongFlags` as to other flag arrays
- **Backwards-compatible**: Existing configs without `knownLongFlags` work identically via auto-derivation

## Usage

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "exec": {
      "safeBins": ["mycli"],
      "safeBinProfiles": {
        "mycli": {
          "knownLongFlags": ["--verbose", "--version", "--help"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Test Plan

- [x] All 96 tests in `exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` pass (90 existing + 6 new)
- [x] All 90 tests across related test files pass (`exec-approvals-safe-bins`, `exec-safe-bin-runtime-policy`, `io.compat`, `doctor/exec-safe-bins`, `doctor-config-flow.safe-bins`, `pi-tools.safe-bins`)
- [x] Generated schema test passes
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (oxlint, type checking, conflict markers)

target.title: fix(hooks): restore --exclude-labels in Gmail watcher args
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #56635

- Restores `--exclude-labels SPAM,TRASH,DRAFT,SENT` in `gog gmail watch serve` args so sent mail and draft saves do not trigger Gmail hook runs by default.
- Adds `hooks.gmail.excludeLabels` to the config schema/types with runtime default resolution and hook override support.
- Updates the sanitized log-args expectation so `--exclude-labels` appears in non-secret watcher logs.
- Regenerates the base config schema and docs baseline hash, and documents the default excluded label set.

AI-assisted: yes (Codex). I reviewed the diff and verified the behavior locally.

target.title: feat: fire session_end hook on subagent completion and cron session end
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the `session_end` plugin hook to fire in two additional lifecycle cases that were previously missing.

### Problem

The `session_end` hook only fired on session reset/replace (one narrow path in `session.ts`). This made it impossible for plugins to react to task completion without polling — blocking use cases like dispatch queues, memory consolidation, and post-session cleanup.

Tracked in:
- Fixes #10142 (session:end event for workflow integration)
- Related to #31266 (session-memory hook wanting session:end)

### Changes

**`src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts`**
- Fires `session_end` alongside the existing `subagent_ended` hook when a subagent completes
- Guarded with `hookRunner?.hasHooks('session_end')` — zero overhead if no listeners
- Uses `buildSessionEndHookPayload` for consistent payload shape

**`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`**
- Fires `session_end` after the cron isolated agent session completes and session entry is persisted
- Same guard pattern

**`src/plugins/types.ts`**
- Updated `session_end` comment to document all three trigger cases: session reset/replace, subagent completion, cron completion

**`CHANGELOG.md`**
- Added entry under Unreleased

### Backward Compatibility

- `subagent_ended` hook continues to fire alongside `session_end` — no breaking change
- All new calls are opt-in via the existing `hasHooks` guard

### Motivation

This unblocks the [openclaw-dispatch](https://clawhub.com) plugin (currently in development) which needs to know when sessions complete to drain a task queue without heartbeat polling.

target.title: fix: non-interactive paste-token and PAT auth for github-copilot provider
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw cannot be deployed in fully automated environments (Terraform, CI/CD, Docker, NVIDIA OpenShell sandboxes) because two critical auth paths require an interactive TTY:

1. **`paste-token` has no `--token` flag** — the command always prompts interactively via `@clack/prompts text()`, with no way to pass the token value non-interactively. The only workaround is manually writing `auth-profiles.json`, but the schema is undocumented and fragile.

2. **`github-copilot` provider rejects PATs with 403** — the token exchange endpoint (`/copilot_internal/v2/token`) only accepts OAuth tokens (`ghu_`). When a PAT (`github_pat_`, `ghp_`) is passed with `Authorization: Bearer ...`, the endpoint returns HTTP 403. The only working path is the interactive device flow (`login-github-copilot`), which requires a browser.

Combined, there is **zero non-interactive path** to configure the Copilot provider — blocking fully automated deployments.

## Context

These issues were discovered while building [Terraform IaC for automated OpenClaw deployment](https://github.com/htekdev/openclaw-repo-management) inside NVIDIA OpenShell sandboxes. The entire stack (EC2/Azure VM, Docker, OpenShell, sandbox, OpenClaw, Telegram) can be automated via `terraform apply` — except Copilot auth, which required manually SSH-ing in.

After 20+ hours of debugging (documented in [session-report.md](https://github.com/htekdev/openclaw-repo-management/blob/main/docs/session-report.md)), we found that:

- PATs **can** call the Copilot API directly — they don't need the `/v2/token` JWT exchange at all
- The [Azure/az-prototype Copilot provider](https://github.com/Azure/az-prototype/blob/main/azext_prototype/ai/copilot_provider.py) confirms this: it sends raw tokens directly to `api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com` with editor-identification headers, no exchange step
- The enterprise endpoint exposes the full model catalogue (Claude, GPT, Gemini) vs the individual endpoint

## Changes

### Fix 1: `paste-token --token` flag

**`src/cli/models-cli.ts`** — Added `--token <value>` option to the CLI command

**`src/commands/models/auth.ts`** — Three non-interactive token sources (in priority order):
1. `--token <value>` — direct flag
2. `--token -` — read from stdin (pipe-friendly, token not in process args)
3. `OPENCLAW_PASTE_TOKEN` env var (not visible in `ps`)
4. Falls back to interactive prompt when TTY is available

**`src/commands/models/auth.test.ts`** — 6 new test cases

### Fix 2: PAT auth for github-copilot

**`extensions/github-copilot/token.ts`**:
- `isGitHubPAT()` — detect `github_pat_` / `ghp_` prefixes
- `resolveCopilotApiToken()` — when PAT detected, skip `/v2/token` exchange entirely and return the raw token with the enterprise base URL
- Export `COPILOT_EDITOR_HEADERS` (Copilot-Integration-Id, Editor-Version, Editor-Plugin-Version) — required for PAT auth
- Export `ENTERPRISE_COPILOT_API_BASE_URL` (`api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com`)
- OAuth tokens (`ghu_`) are unchanged — still go through `/v2/token` exchange

**`extensions/github-copilot/index.ts`** — When a PAT is detected, inject `COPILOT_EDITOR_HEADERS` into the provider config so they're sent with every API request

**`extensions/github-copilot/token.test.ts`** — 4 new test cases

## Usage

```bash
# Automated deployment (Terraform user-data, CI/CD, Docker, OpenShell sandbox)
openclaw models auth paste-token --provider github-copilot --token "$COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN"

# Stdin (pipe from secret manager)
vault kv get -field=token secret/copilot | openclaw models auth paste-token --provider github-copilot --token -

# Env var
export OPENCLAW_PASTE_TOKEN="github_pat_..."
openclaw models auth paste-token --provider github-copilot

# Interactive (unchanged)
openclaw models auth paste-token --provider github-copilot
```

## Notes

- Supersedes #52509 (same changes, cleaner branch history from main)

Fixes #52321
Fixes #52322

target.title: Add time-based session maintenance modes
target.body:
## Summary
- add time-based session maintenance settings using `sessionCacheTtl` plus `timeBasedContextCompact`
- support explicit `compact` and `reset` maintenance modes instead of implicitly coupling both behaviors together
- track session idle/cache timestamps and run gateway maintenance sweeps to apply the selected policy

## Why
This change adds two time-based session maintenance workflows for long-lived sessions:

1. `compact`
   - Purpose: reduce the chance of a cold cache miss after a long idle period by proactively compacting an idle session shortly before the configured timeout.
   - Good fit: long research or coding threads where the user may come back later and continue from the same context.

2. `reset`
   - Purpose: aggressively clear stale sessions once they have been idle past the configured timeout instead of carrying old context forward.
   - Good fit: workflows where stale context is more harmful than helpful, or where keeping expired sessions around only increases cost and surprise.

The new behavior is controlled explicitly per model:
- `sessionCacheTtl` defines the timeout window
- `timeBasedContextCompact` chooses the maintenance algorithm: `none`, `compact`, or `reset`

`cacheRetention` remains a model prompt-cache setting and is no longer used to enable or disable these new maintenance features.

## Implementation notes
- keep Anthropic API-key smart defaults at `cacheRetention: "short"`
- add `sessionCacheTtl: "1h"` as a defaulted timeout value without auto-enabling maintenance mode
- warn when `sessionCacheTtl` exceeds an explicitly configured model cache TTL
- record `lastUserMessageAt`, `lastAssistantMessageAt`, `lastCacheTouchAt`, and `lastIdleCompactionForCacheTouchAt` in session state
- run the maintenance sweep from gateway server maintenance without overlapping runs

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/session-cache-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/config/config.pruning-defaults.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/cache-ttl.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.cache-retention-default.test.ts src/commands/agent/session-store.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit`

target.title: fix(plugins): dedupe tool registration and preserve plugin tool config
target.body:
## Summary
- dedupe repeated per-plugin tool registration in the registry instead of patching Feishu locally
- preserve `plugins.entries.<id>.config` when plugin tool factories are invoked
- reuse a compatible gateway-bindable active registry for default runtime requests without widening reuse across unrelated load contexts

## Why
- addresses the root cause behind repeated Feishu tool registration during startup (#56494)
- integrates the plugin tool factory context fix from #56474
